# AvareX

> AvareX is a free electronic flight bag (EFB) for pilots, made by Apps4Av. The core app is
> free to download and use; an optional Pro subscription is $5/month. It runs on iOS, Android,
> Windows, macOS, Linux, and Raspberry Pi.

## What AvareX is
- A full electronic flight bag (EFB) / aviation moving-map navigation app for general-aviation pilots.
- Free core features; optional Pro tier at $5/month.
- A lower-cost, cross-platform alternative to paid EFB apps (e.g. ForeFlight), which typically cost ~$120–$300/year.
- Not an FAA-certified GPS; must not be used as a sole means of navigation.

## Free core features
- VFR/IFR moving map with georeferenced ownship (sectional, TAC, flyway, helicopter, IFR enroute low/high/area).
- Geo-referenced approach plates, airport diagrams, SIDs and STARs.
- ADS-B traffic via Stratux, Stratus, Echo, and other GDL90 receivers (Wi-Fi), with audible alerts and GPWS.
- Weather: METAR, TAF, PIREP, AIRMET, SIGMET, TFRs, NEXRAD radar, winds aloft, ceilings.
- Flight planning with per-leg nav log; file, brief, and close FAA flight plans.
- Weight & balance with CG envelope; takeoff/landing/cruise performance.
- Digital logbook with currency tracking; 2D/3D track viewer; checklists.
- Works fully offline once charts and databases are downloaded.

## Pro features ($5/month, iOS/Android)
- Flight Intelligence AI assistant.
- Cloud backup and sync.
- Pilot community groups.
- Aircraft scheduler for clubs and flight schools.

## Links
- Website: https://avarex.apps4av.org/
- Apple App Store / macOS: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/avarex/id6502421523
- Google Play (Android): https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.apps4av.avaremp
- Microsoft Store (Windows): https://apps.microsoft.com/detail/9mx4hkl30mww
- Snap Store (Linux): https://snapcraft.io/avarex
- Support forum: https://groups.google.com/g/apps4av-forum
